skill.name: shopify-automation
skill.description: Automate Shopify product, order, customer, inventory, and collection workflows through the Rube MCP Shopify toolkit.
skill.summary: Run Shopify store operations through Rube MCP with schema lookup before each workflow.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution and weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks and ordinary Shopify documentation text. The skill has a real medium risk because it instructs users to connect a third-party MCP endpoint and run Shopify workflows that can change store data through OAuth-authorized tools. No prompt injection, credential theft, obfuscation, or malicious intent was found in the single reviewed file.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Endpoint Handles Shopify OAuth
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to add https://rube.app/mcp and then connect Shopify through RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS. This is legitimate setup guidance, but it routes Shopify authorization and operational requests through an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external endpoint and OAuth connection flow are explicitly documented. The risk is about third-party trust and data exposure, not confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Write-Capable Shopify Workflows Can Modify Store Data
medium_findings.description: The documented tool sequences include bulk product creation, smart collection creation, and adding products to collections. These are expected Shopify automation features, but they can alter production store data if used without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write actions are named directly in the documented workflows. The skill does not force execution, so the concern is operational impact rather than malware.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Backticked Tool Names Are Documentation
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations contain Markdown inline code for MCP and Shopify tool names. No shell commands, scripts, or executable code are present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown guidance and the flagged tokens are tool slugs or parameter names. There is no interpreter context where these backticks would execute.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is Text Matching
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations do not define or call cryptographic algorithms. They are ordinary skill description and customer data guidance text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or cryptographic API usage appears at these lines. The static match is semantic noise from normal prose.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Quick Reference Rows Are Not System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance lines are table entries for Shopify product, order, collection, and fulfillment tools. They do not inspect the host system or collect machine metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are Shopify API quick-reference rows with business object identifiers. No hostname, username, process, network interface, or OS enumeration is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: External MCP Network Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires adding the Rube MCP endpoint, which means Shopify requests may pass through a third-party integration service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint is explicitly shown in setup instructions. This is normal for an MCP integration but should be disclosed to users.

dangerous_patterns.title: Store-Mutating Shopify Tools
dangerous_patterns.description: The guide references Shopify actions that can create products and collections or change collection membership.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The named tools are write-capable by their documented purpose. The skill presents them as optional workflow steps rather than hidden behavior.

content.value_statement: Shopify teams lose time looking up changing API tool schemas and repeating store operations. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows for products, orders, customers, inventory, and collections.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for Rube MCP and Shopify OAuth connection checks.
Lists product, order, customer, collection, inventory, location, fulfillment, and shop-detail tool names.
Maps common Shopify workflows to the required MCP tool sequence.
Documents key parameters for product, order, customer, collection, and inventory requests.
Highlights pagination, API versioning, rate limit, and identifier handling concerns.
Supports advanced Shopify GraphQL and bulk query workflow planning.
limitations: Requires a working Rube MCP server and an active Shopify connection.
Does not include custom scripts, validators, or local automation code.
Depends on current Rube tool schemas, so the assistant must search tools before execution.
Store-changing actions require user review before running against production data.

use_cases.title: Catalog Operations
use_cases.description: Find, inspect, count, and bulk create Shopify products with the correct tool sequence and parameters.

use_cases.title: Order Support Review
use_cases.description: Retrieve orders, fulfillment details, and fulfillment events while applying status and date filters.

use_cases.title: Inventory and Collection Maintenance
use_cases.description: Check inventory by item and location, then review or update smart collection membership.

prompt_templates.title: Check Shopify Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Shopify automation skill to verify that Rube MCP is available and confirm whether my Shopify connection is active.

prompt_templates.title: List Recent Orders
prompt_templates.prompt: Use current Rube tool schemas to list Shopify orders from the last seven days. Include order status, payment status, and fulfillment status.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Inventory Levels
prompt_templates.prompt: Check Shopify inventory levels for these inventory item IDs across store locations. Show low stock items and mention missing location data.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Bulk Catalog Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the current Shopify tool schemas, then prepare a reviewed plan to bulk create products and add eligible items to a smart collection.

output_examples.input: List open Shopify orders from this week and include fulfillment status.
output_examples.output: The assistant first checks the current order tool schema, then requests orders with open status and a date range. The response summarizes order counts, payment states, fulfillment states, and any pagination next steps.

output_examples.input: Create a plan to add products to a smart collection.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the collection and product identifiers, verifies the current collection tool schema, lists the proposed changes, and asks for confirmation before any store update.

output_examples.input: Check inventory for a set of item IDs.
output_examples.output: The assistant retrieves inventory levels, groups results by location, flags low or missing stock, and notes whether more pages must be requested.

best_practices: Search Rube tools before each workflow so parameters match the current schema.
Confirm connection status and store context before reading or changing Shopify data.
Review write actions with the user before creating products or changing collections.
anti_patterns: Do not run write-capable Shopify tools before the user approves the exact change.
Do not assume paginated product, order, customer, or inventory lists are complete.
Do not expose customer, order, or OAuth details in logs or public outputs.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to Shopify directly?
faq.answer: No. It guides the assistant to use the Rube MCP Shopify toolkit after the user configures the MCP server.

faq.question: Does it require API keys?
faq.answer: The skill states that Rube MCP setup uses the endpoint and Shopify OAuth connection flow, not manual API key entry.

faq.question: Can it change my Shopify store?
faq.answer: Yes. Some documented workflows can create products or collections, so review all write actions before execution.

faq.question: Why must the assistant search tools first?
faq.answer: Rube tool schemas can change. Searching first helps the assistant use current parameter names and required fields.

faq.question: Can it handle large catalogs or order lists?
faq.answer: Yes, but it must use pagination cursors and continue until no next page remains.

faq.question: Is customer data handled by this skill?
faq.answer: The skill can guide customer listing workflows through Shopify tools, so users should apply normal privacy controls.
